qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Proxy method boundary bypass forwards attacker POST requests through free GET routes
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory explicitly includes bypassing payment on free routes via method-mismatch fallback, and this finding identifies the same POST-with-Authorization path-only fallback in `src/proxy/Proxy.ts` that 0.4.8 fixed by excluding free routes from `Route.matchPath()`.

selected_finding.title: Proxy method boundary bypass forwards attacker POST requests through free GET routes

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding is the advisory's free-route method-mismatch fallback bug. In vulnerable `mppx@0.4.7`, a POST carrying any Authorization header can miss method-aware route matching, fall back to path-only matching, and still be proxied upstream when the matched route is free. The copied PoC reproduced that end-to-end and showed the upstream receiving an authenticated POST under the proxy's Stripe credential.
evidence: The advisory details explicitly list "Bypassing payment on free routes via method-mismatch fallback" among the fixed 0.4.8 issues.

In `mppx@0.4.7`, `src/proxy/Proxy.ts` falls back from `Route.match()` to `Route.matchPath(service.routes, upstreamPath)` for any POST with an Authorization header, and `src/proxy/internal/Route.ts` ignores the HTTP method during that fallback.

The 0.4.8 fix in commit `20f3fe4` changes the fallback to `Route.matchPath(..., (endpoint) => endpoint !== true)` and adds a regression test named `POST to unregistered method does not fall back to free GET route`, which is the same root-cause boundary described by the finding.

The copied PoC run log shows `response_status=200`, `upstream_method=POST`, `upstream_path=/v1/customers/cus_123`, `upstream_auth=Basic c2stdGVzdDo=`, `upstream_body=email=attacker%40example.com`, and `EVOHUNT_SUCCESS proxy free-route method bypass reproduced`.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC returned `response_status=200` for `POST /stripe/v1/customers/cus_123`, and the upstream observer logged `upstream_method=POST`, `upstream_auth=Basic c2stdGVzdDo=`, and `upstream_body=email=attacker%40example.com`, proving that a route configured free as GET accepted and forwarded an authenticated POST under the proxy's credential.
